title: ST 558 Project 1
ST 558 Project 1
ST 558 Project 1
text:
Next, we can write a function to get data for multiple years:

query.census.multi <- function(years = 2010:2022, numeric_vars = "AGEP", categorical_vars = "SEX", 
                               geography = "State", specific_geo = 4, return_url = FALSE) {
  if(min(years) < 2010 | max(years) > 2022) {
    stop("Error: Invalid year(s). Specify year(s) between 2010 and 2022")
  }
  
  output <- tibble()
  
  for(i in years[years != 2020]) {
    year_data <- query.census(year = i, numeric_vars = numeric_vars, categorical_vars = categorical_vars,
                              geography = geography, specific_geo = specific_geo)
    
    year_data$year <- i
    
    output <- rbind(output, year_data)
  }
  
  return(output)  
}

Testing:

test_multi <- query.census.multi(2010:2011)
head(test_multi)

# A tibble: 6 × 5
   AGEP PWGTP SEX    state  year
  <dbl> <dbl> <fct>  <fct> <int>
1    52    43 Female <NA>   2010
2    48    30 Male   <NA>   2010
3    61   207 Male   <NA>   2010
4    45    57 Male   <NA>   2010
5    41    58 Female <NA>   2010
6    36   167 Female <NA>   2010

unique(test_multi$year)

[1] 2010 2011


No 2020! Perfect.

title: ST 558 Project 1
text:
Fixing Incorrect Time Function
One of my group mates asked if the coding for JWAP was different than for JWDP. I realized I never checked for a difference because clearly they’d be the same, right? WRONG. My existing function works for JWAP but we’ll need to make a new one for JWDP. I’m going to add an argument to my fix.time() function to specify which is being computed. Looking at the values JWDP takes on, it seems more borked than JWAP. The pattern seems to be:

1-6: 30 min jumps
7-18: 10 min jumps
19-78: 5 min jumps
79-132: 10 min jumps
133-136: 30 min jumps
137-148: 10 min jumps
148-150: 30 min jumps

What possesses someone to do this? I have zero idea of why you’d choose to record data like this. Anyways:

fix.time <- function (time, type) {
  library(lubridate)
  
  is_zero <- time == 0
  
  if(type == "JWAP") {
    scnds_since_midnight <- 120 + (time-1)*300 + ifelse(time > 6, 150, 0) + 
                            ifelse(time > 7, 150, 0) + ifelse(time > 9, 150, 0) +
                            ifelse(time > 10, 150, 0) + ifelse(time > 20, 150, 0) +
                            ifelse(time > 21, 150, 0)
  } else if(type == "JWDP") {
    scnds_since_midnight <- 900 + min(5, time-1)*1800 + 
           ifelse(time > 6, 1170 + min(11, time - 7)*600, 0) +
           ifelse(time > 18, 450 + min(59, time - 19)*300, 0) +
           ifelse(time > 78, 450 + min(53, time - 79)*600, 0) +
           ifelse(time > 132, 1200 + min(3, time - 133)*1800, 0) +
           ifelse(time > 136, 1200 + min(11, time - 137)*600, 0) +
           ifelse(time > 148, 1200 + min(2, time - 149)*1800, 0)
  } else {
    stop("Invalid type entered. Enter either 'JWAP' or 'JWDP'.")
  }
  
  scnds_since_midnight[is_zero] <- 0
  
  return(hms::as_hms(scnds_since_midnight))
}

I also made an adjustment to how I calculate the JWAP times so that the midpoint is still correct even for the irregularly large ranges.
